title: Systems and methods for performing and tracking asset inspections
abstract: A facility readiness inspection system and method are disclosed. Embodiments comprise an asset database comprising inspection task definition for assets. An inspection coordination application program interface (API) can create at least one periodic inspection task list. Each periodic inspection task list can include a number of tasks and assets, chosen based on the total time allotted for completion of the periodic inspection task list and the complexity of the inspection task definitions of each physical asset. Mobile electronic device can be configured to present the periodic inspection task list as a series of discrete tasks and to receive task list response data in response to each discrete task. Response data can be compared to benchmarks and standards, and issues can be escalated to external systems. An analytics engine can automatically produce inspection compliance documentation for each of the physical assets related to the periodic inspection task list.
claims:
Claims claimed is: A facility readiness inspection system comprising: an asset database comprising an asset identification, an asset location, at least one asset inspection task definition, and an asset inspection frequency for a plurality of physical assets at a facility; an inspection coordination application program interface (API) communicatively coupled with the asset database and configured to create at least one periodic inspection task list, each periodic inspection task list including a number of asset identifications, the number being related to a total time allotted for completion of the at least one periodic inspection task list and an aggregate complexity of the asset inspection task definitions of physical assets in the periodic inspection task list, and the periodicity of the inspection task list being related at least in part to the asset inspection frequency of at least one of the plurality of physical assets; at least one mobile electronic device, each mobile electronic device configured to host an instance of the inspection coordination API to present a different one of the at least one periodic inspection task lists in a user interface, the user interface configured to present the periodic inspection task list as a series of discrete tasks and to receive task list response data in response to each discrete task; and an inspection analytics engine communicatively coupled with the inspection coordination API and the at least one mobile electronic device and configured to: receive the task list response data and task list completion metadata from the at least one mobile electronic device, 

 compare the task list completion metadata with benchmark data to determine whether the periodic inspection task list was completed according to at least one internal predefined standard, and automatically produce inspection compliance documentation for each of the physical assets related to the periodic inspection task list. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the received task list response data comprises sensor data collected as directed by the periodic inspection task list by at least one sensor of the at least one mobile electronic device. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the at least one sensor comprises any of an optical sensor, a temperature sensor, a pressure sensor, a position sensor, or an infrared sensor. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein the task list completion metadata comprises at least one of a time when a particular task in the periodic inspection task list was completed or a geolocation where a particular task in the periodic inspection task list was completed. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein the inspection analytics engine is further configured to automatically produce a maintenance request if the received task list response data includes a maintenance indicator. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein at least one of the mobile electronic devices is configured to present a managerial instance of the inspection coordination API, the managerial instance 

 comprising at least one of a status dashboard of in-progress periodic inspection task lists, a visualization of task list completion metadata aggregated according to at least one factor, or a visualized comparison of the task list completion metadata and the benchmark data. 7. The system of claim 6, wherein the at least one factor is a common user, an area, a department, a type of physical asset, a date, a time, or a period of time. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the automatically produced inspection compliance documentation is formatted for at least one of an internal standard or an external standard. 9. The system of claim 8, wherein the external standard is a government standard. 10. The system of claim 1, wherein the at least one internal predefined standard is an acceptable range of time for completing the periodic inspection task list. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein the inspection analytics engine is further configured to compare the task list completion metadata with benchmark data to determine whether an individual task in the periodic inspection task list was completed according to at least one internal predefined task standard. 12. The system of claim 11, wherein the at least one internal predefined task standard is an acceptable range of time for completing the individual task. 13. The system of claim 1, wherein the asset inspection frequency is defined by a legal requirement. 14. The system of claim 1, wherein the an inspection coordination API is configured to consider relative asset locations of the physical assets in the facility to create the at least one periodic inspection task list. 15. A method of managing facility readiness inspections comprising: defining a plurality of assets for inspection in a facility, each asset definition comprising an asset identification, an asset location, at least one asset inspection task definition, and an asset inspection frequency; building at least one periodic inspection task list, each periodic inspection task list including a number of asset identifications, the number being related to a total time allotted for completion of the at least one periodic inspection task list and an aggregate complexity of the asset inspection task definitions of physical assets in the periodic inspection task list, and the periodicity of the inspection task list being related at least in part to the asset inspection frequency of at least one of the plurality of physical assets; dynamically presenting the at least one periodic inspection task list as a series of discrete tasks in an electronic user interface; receiving task list response data and task list completion metadata in response to each discrete task; comparing the task list completion metadata with benchmark data to determine whether 

 the periodic inspection task list was completed according to at least one internal predefined standard; and automatically producing inspection compliance documentation for each of the physical assets related to the periodic inspection task list. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein receiving task list response data comprises receiving sensor data collected as directed by the periodic inspection task list by at least one sensor. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the at least one sensor comprises any of an optical sensor, a temperature sensor, a pressure sensor, a position sensor, or an infrared sensor. 18. The method of claim 15, wherein the task list completion metadata comprises at least one of a time when a particular task in the periodic inspection task list was completed or a geolocation where a particular task in the periodic inspection task list was completed. 19. The method of claim 15, further comprising automatically producing a maintenance request if the received task list response data includes a maintenance indicator. 20. The method of claim 15, further comprising dynamically presenting a managerial instance of the inspection coordination API, the managerial instance comprising at least one of a status dashboard of in-progress periodic inspection task lists, a visualization of task list completion metadata aggregated according to at least one factor, or a visualized comparison of the task list completion metadata and the benchmark data. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the at least one factor is a common user, an area, a department, a type of physical asset, a date, a time, or a period of time. 22. The method of claim 15, wherein automatically producing inspection compliance documentation further comprises formatting the inspection compliance documentation for at least one of an internal standard or an external standard. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the external standard is a government standard. 24. The method of claim 15, wherein the at least one internal predefined standard is an acceptable range of time for completing the periodic inspection task list. 25. The method of claim 24, further comprising comparing the task list completion metadata with benchmark data to determine whether an individual task in the periodic inspection task list was completed according to at least one internal predefined task standard. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the at least one internal predefined task standard is an acceptable range of time for completing the individual task. 27. The method of claim 15, wherein the asset inspection frequency is defined by a legal requirement. 28. The method of claim 15, wherein the building further comprises considering relative asset locations of the physical assets in the facility to create the at least one periodic inspection task list.
